name: BTC Value Reacceptance Exhaustion v1
thesis: BTC 1h is the best similar-asset lane for testing mean reversion from exhaustion only when structure reaccepts prior value. The edge should come from exhaustion plus value reacceptance, not from blindly fading momentum.
edge_mechanism: exhaustion mean reversion into value reacceptance
invalidation_condition: Abort if exhaustion-plus-reacceptance does not generate enough trades, if value re-entry fails to mean revert, or if the lane is too trend-dominated for fading to survive costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price stretches away from short-term value, exhausts, and becomes tradable only once it re-enters balance rather than continuing to trend.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: A mostly choppy brief should reward structured fades into value more than breakout chasing, but the reacceptance rule avoids catching every knife.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 1h is a clean similar-asset lane where value reacceptance is often visible and intraday noise is lower than on high-beta alts.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If BTC runs in clean directional sequences, exhaustion stretches may persist and punish the fade even after reacceptance.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If reversion works but overextends less than expected, tighten profit-taking rather than adding more entry filters.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; stretch and value reacceptance are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by using moderate stretch thresholds rather than extreme capitulation-only conditions

long: Low <= EMA_20 - 0.55 * ATR_14 OR Low <= LOWER_BOLLINGER_20_2
short: High >= EMA_20 + 0.55 * ATR_14 OR High >= UPPER_BOLLINGER_20_2

long: Final 20% exits on touch of EMA_20 + 0.4 * ATR_14 or 4 bars
short: Final 20% exits on touch of EMA_20 - 0.4 * ATR_14 or 4 bars
